reasoning: The Lean signature `generateFrom A = generateFrom (sInter {T | is_topology X T ∧ A ⊆ T})` is logically equivalent to the informal claim. The RHS `sInter S` (intersection of all topologies containing A) is itself a topology, since topology axioms are preserved under arbitrary intersection of collections of open sets. Thus `generateFrom (sInter S)` equals the topology whose opens are `sInter S` (via `generateFrom_setOf_isOpen`), so the Lean equality is equivalent to "open sets of `generateFrom A` = `sInter S`", which is exactly what the informal asserts. The "subbasis" framing in Munkres adds at most a covering condition ⋃₀A = X, but this is not actually used in the conclusion — the claim holds for arbitrary collections A — so omitting it is not a faithfulness loss. The GT version (proofnet-292) uses the identical signature.
